title: fraxinus potosina
intro: Fraxinus potosina, also known as the Potosi Ash, is a species of tree native to Mexico and Central America. This tree has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds that have been used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus potosina are believed to provide relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Traditionally, herbal preparations of the tree's bark have been made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
